title: Quick check: Revalidation and optimistic UI

questions.prompt: Difference between revalidatePath and revalidateTag — when to use each?

choices.text: `revalidatePath(path)` invalidates the cache for a specific route — useful when you know the exact path that needs refreshing (`revalidatePath('/posts')`). `revalidateTag(tag)` invalidates every fetch tagged with that key — useful when one mutation affects many paths that all share a common data source. Tag your fetches with `next: { tags: ['posts'] }`, then `revalidateTag('posts')` invalidates the homepage, /posts, profile pages, anywhere with that tag. Tags are cleaner when one mutation affects many paths; paths are direct when you know exactly which routes need refreshing.

questions.explanation: Path = specific route. Tag = everywhere that data is fetched. Use tags for cross-cutting invalidation.

questions.prompt: Adding to a todo list should feel instant but persist to DB. Pattern?

choices.text: useOptimistic — wrap the items array with `const [optimisticItems, addOptimistic] = useOptimistic(items, (current, newItem) => [...current, newItem])`. When the user adds: call `addOptimistic(newItem)` to update UI instantly, then await the Server Action. On success, the Server Action revalidates and the real items arrive (replacing the optimistic). On failure, React reverts to the original. The user sees the new item immediately; the server catches up. Pair with a Server Action that calls `revalidatePath` or `revalidateTag` to bring fresh data after the mutation.

questions.explanation: useOptimistic for instant UI; Server Action revalidates; auto-revert on failure.
